objective: Assess understanding of comparing fractions: same denominator, same numerator, equivalent fractions, ordering fractions

questions.question: Compare: 3/8 ○ 5/8
hints: Same denominator — compare numerators.

questions.question: Which fraction is greater: 4/6 or 2/6?

questions.question: Which TWO fractions are equivalent to 1/2?
hints: 1/2 = 2/4 = 3/6. Each names the same amount.

questions.question: Jake says 3/4 > 5/6 because 4 < 6. Is Jake correct? Why?
choices: Yes — smaller denominator always means greater fraction
No — you cannot compare fractions with different numerators AND denominators this way
Yes — 3/4 is always greater than any fraction with 6 in the denominator
hints: Jake's rule only works when numerators are the same. Use a number line to compare 3/4 and 5/6.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.9 Unit Assessment 'Compare Fractions'
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 5 Topic G End-of-Module — Compare Fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

questions.question: Three friends ran different distances: Ana ran 3/4 mile, Ben ran 2/3 mile, and Cara ran 5/6 mile. Who ran the farthest?
hints: Compare each to the benchmark 1 whole: 5/6 is closest to 1.
